narrative.headline: What 2017 Honda Accord owners report most
narrative.summary: Electrical system faults are the most commonly reported problem, with owners describing unexpected behavior from vehicle controls while the car is parked and unoccupied. Engine and fuel/propulsion complaints follow, including reports of stalling, fire risk, and a widely noted lag in throttle response when accelerating from a stop. Service brake reports center on the collision-detection system triggering unintended hard braking in active traffic.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report that windows and the sunroof open on their own while the vehicle is parked and the key is not in the ignition. · 6 fire · 5 crash · 3 injured

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report engine-related incidents occurring while the vehicle is parked and shut off, including situations where the car could not be left safely. · 3 fire · 1 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: Owners and dealers report a known acceleration lag in which the vehicle fails to respond promptly when accelerating from a stationary or slow-moving position. · 1 fire · 2 crash

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Owners report the collision-detection system falsely triggering and slamming the brakes to the floor in the presence of oncoming traffic, including mid-intersection events. · 5 crash · 1 injured
